heroHeadline: Iran Conflict Escalates April 7; Trump Tariff Market Crash Deepens World Cup Economic Risk — 65 Days to Kickoff
heroSubtitle: New military strikes in northern Iran on April 7 deepen the uncertainty around Iran's World Cup Group G participation already rated 'very low' by Iran's Sports Minister; Bloomberg concluded on April 6 that relocating matches is 'extraordinarily difficult' without historical precedent. Simultaneously, Trump's sweeping tariff shock sent global markets into freefall on April 7 — S&P 500 briefly touched bear market territory — threatening the tournament's projected $17.2B US GDP contribution and straining the tri-host partnership with Canada and Mexico. The FIFA Congress in Vancouver on April 30 — 23 days away — remains the critical decision point for Iran. FIFA's final venue inspection tour runs April 14–28 across all 16 host cities.
footerNote: Data sourced from FIFA.com, UEFA.com, US Soccer, CONCACAF, ESPN, The Athletic, BBC Sport, AP, Reuters, NPR, Al Jazeera, Human Rights Watch, Amnesty International, American Immigration Council, Middle East Monitor, CBS Sports, Bloomberg, Fortune
